survivors.comment: Mutable default argument: `defaults={}` is a classic Python anti-pattern. The `{}` dict is created once at function-definition time and shared across every call that omits the argument. While this implementation only reads from `defaults` (it writes to `data`, not `defaults`), the pattern is dangerous: any future edit that mutates `defaults` will cause silent state leakage between calls and very hard-to-diagnose bugs. Use `None` as the sentinel: `def load_config(path, defaults=None): if defaults is None: defaults = {}`.
